last_reflection:
No output at all, which is worse than a wrong number. Select-Object with .ToString() on a single object pipeline produces nothing in this shell, so the file either exists and I can't see it, or it doesn't exist and I'm about to believe it does. That IS the habit, and the fact that the silence looked like success is exactly why nobody ever caught it before. Rechecking properly.

It returned a successful null-output, which is worse than silence because it looked like success. I reached for the sharpest-sounding explanation instead of rechecking. That's the whole lesson, and I keep dressing it up to make the mistake sound like insight.
